statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simplify a complex fraction, we find the common denominator of all fractions. In this case, it is {\left(y+3\right)\mathopen{}\left(y-3\right)}. We multiply the complex fractions numerator and denominator by this common denominator, and we have:
Since y+3 and y-3 were canceled, y \ne 3 and y \ne -3 are lost domain conditions.
